page: Amazon Web Services r3.xlarge is a memory-optimized third-generation server instance. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is allocated 4 dedicated vCPUs, which correspond to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of an 80 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps. The instance runs on the Xen hypervisor and does not include any GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the r3.xlarge exhibits limited processing capabilities by modern standards. In stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, performance falls into the weak category, representing the bottom 25% of tested servers. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are rated as poor, falling into the bottom 10%. Memory performance is mixed; while the PassMark memory score achieves an average rating in the middle 50%, memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks fall into the weak category. Cryptographic and compression workloads, such as gzip and PassMark encryption tests, also register in the bottom 25%, while Redis SET operations and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the poor category.

The primary tradeoff of the r3.xlarge is its legacy hardware profile, which delivers lower compute efficiency relative to newer generations. However, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a qualitative cost advantage for legacy workloads requiring significant memory capacity without demanding high CPU throughput. Given its performance profile, the instance is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as basic database operations, caching layers with moderate throughput requirements, and legacy web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r3.xlarge is a memory-optimized legacy instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 30.0 GB of RAM, an 80 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 1 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark evaluations place its CPU performance in the bottom 25% and its Geekbench scores in the bottom 10%. However, its PassMark memory score remains average, highlighting its memory-centric design. Lacking modern instruction sets like AVX-512, this Xen-hypervised instance presents a clear tradeoff between high memory capacity and limited compute power. It is best utilized for legacy memory-intensive applications, moderate database operations, and caching workloads that do not require high CPU throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r3.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 30.0 GB of RAM, and an 80 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for legacy memory-intensive workloads with moderate throughput.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r3.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 30.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized legacy instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe storage for memory-centric workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 30.0 GB RAM
Bundled 80 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average memory performance paired with weak CPU compute benchmarks
Xen hypervisor architecture with x86_64 instruction support
Suited for legacy caching, moderate database operations, and memory-heavy workloads
